name: Peña Corva and Monte Pilas Loop
name_original: Subida a Monte Pilas — circular desde Quecedo de Valdivieso
description: This difficult 11.3-mile (18.3 km) circular hike to Monte Pilas from Quecedo de Valdivieso offers panoramic views and varied terrain.
region_intro: Hiking around Merindad De Valdivielso is characterized by a valley carved by the Ebro River, featuring both fluvial and mountainous scenery. The region includes dramatic gorges like Los Hocinos and the Hoz gorge, alongside the elevated views of the Sierra de la Tesla. Trails traverse diverse landscapes, from riverside paths to ascents through mountain ranges. This area provides a network of routes connecting natural features with historical sites. Best hiking trails around Merindad De Valdivielso The most popular hiking route is Peña Escalera Trail, a 5.3 miles (8.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 59 minutes to complete. This moderate route features an elevation gain of 1,567 feet (478 m). Another top favourite among local hikers is Peña Corva and Monte Pilas Loop, a difficult 11.4 miles (18.3 km) path. This trail offers significant elevation changes and views of the Sierra de la Tesla. Local hikers also love the RutaDelArte4-Portico – ArtRoute3-Clover loop from Condado, a 7.3 miles (11.8 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 13 minutes. Hiking around Merindad De Valdivielso is defined by deep gorges, the Ebro River valley, and the Sierra de la Tesla mountain range. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate valley walks to more challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Merindad De Valdivielso are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 300 hikers have used komoot to explore Merindad De Valdivielso's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Merindad De Valdivielso?
A: Merindad De Valdivielso offers a diverse network of nearly 20 hiking trails. These routes cater to various preferences, from easy riverside strolls to more challenging mountain ascents through the Sierra de la Tesla.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the hiking trails in Merindad De Valdivielso?
A: The terrain in Merindad De Valdivielso is highly varied, characterized by a blend of fluvial and mountainous scenery. You'll encounter deep gorges like Los Hocinos, riverside paths along the Ebro River, and elevated sections in the Sierra de la Tesla. Trails often consist of dirt tracks and rural roads, providing a natural and immersive experience.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families with children?
A: Yes, Merindad De Valdivielso has several easy hiking trails perfect for beginners or families. There are at least two designated easy routes, and many of the local PR-BU trails, such as the PR-BU 179 Sendero de Pilas or PR-BU 182 Sendero de Peña Escalera, offer gentle gradients and manageable distances. These routes often pass through charming villages and scenic landscapes.

Q: Are there challenging hikes for experienced trekkers in the region?
A: Absolutely. For experienced trekkers, Merindad De Valdivielso offers six difficult routes with significant elevation changes. A notable example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1046571483/pena-corva-and-monte-pilas-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Corva and Monte Pilas Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through the Sierra de la Tesla, offering demanding ascents and panoramic views.

Q: Are there any waterfalls to see on the hiking trails?
A: Yes, the region is home to the beautiful &lt;strong&gt;Cascada de Tartalés de los Montes&lt;/strong&gt;. This scenic waterfall is a highlight for many hikers and can be incorporated into various routes, including a recommended 16.8 km circular route that passes through Tartalés de los Montes.

Q: Can I find historical landmarks or cultural sites along the routes?
A: Merindad De Valdivielso is rich in history. Many trails connect natural features with historical sites. You can explore charming villages with Romanesque churches, the 11th-12th century San Pedro de Tejada abbey, and ancient cave hermitages like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5775442" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;CuevasDeLosMoros&lt;/a&gt;. Other sites include the Torre de los Saravia and the ruins of Castillo de Malvecino.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, Merindad De Valdivielso offers several circular routes. A popular option is a 16.8 km route that passes through villages like Condado, Panizares, and Tartalés, incorporating sights such as the Tartalés waterfall and the Cereceda reservoir. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15000666" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;RutaDelArte4-Portico – ArtRoute3-Clover loop from Condado&lt;/a&gt; is another well-liked circular trail.

Q: What are some notable natural features or viewpoints in the area?
A: The region is renowned for its impressive natural features. The dramatic gorges of Los Hocinos and Hoz offer spectacular views, especially from points like the 'Puente del Aire' near Valdenoceda. The Sierra de la Tesla provides elevated vistas from peaks such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4431203" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Pilas&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5116707" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coronilla Summit&lt;/a&gt;. The Cereceda Reservoir also offers picturesque views with the mountains as a backdrop.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Merindad De Valdivielso?
A: The trails in Merindad De Valdivielso are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 60 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse landscapes, from deep gorges to mountain ranges, and the well-maintained network of paths that connect natural beauty with historical charm. The variety of routes suitable for different skill levels is also a common highlight.

Q: Is it possible to explore caves or mountain peaks in Merindad De Valdivielso?
A: Yes, the area offers opportunities to explore both caves and mountain peaks. You can visit sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4977826" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caves of Penedo de los Portugueses Hermitage&lt;/a&gt; or the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5775442" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;CuevasDeLosMoros&lt;/a&gt;. For peaks, you can ascend &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4431203" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Pilas&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5641331" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;AltoDeTesla&lt;/a&gt; for stunning panoramic views of the surrounding landscape.

Q: Are there any specific long-distance trails that pass through Merindad De Valdivielso?
A: Yes, Merindad De Valdivielso is traversed by two significant long-distance trails: the GR-85 "Ruta de los sentidos" and the GR-99 "Camino natural del Ebro." These trails offer extensive exploration opportunities for those looking for multi-day hikes or longer sections of regional routes.
tips: Impressive and steep gorge. It also has the cave of Las Pilas in the gorge itself to the left to complete the visit

Mountain that exceeds more than one thousand two hundred meters and that offers impressive views of the entire Sierra de Tesla. It is a table-shaped mountain with cliffs on the southern side of its summit and close to other peaks in the area, such as Peña Corba.

Beautiful and small town located in the Sierra de Tesla and offering a core surrounded by mountains that rise on curiously shaped rocks. In the northern part of the town we find the Peña de San Martín, a rocky line that creates a natural wall of gray rock.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the Ascent to Monte Pilas circular hike?
faqs.answer: The trail typically starts and ends in the village of Quecedo de Valdivielso. You should be able to find parking within or near the village to begin your hike.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for dogs?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Ascent to Monte Pilas is generally dog-friendly. However, given the trail's difficult rating, significant elevation gain, and varied terrain, including potentially steep and exposed sections, ensure your dog is fit and accustomed to challenging hikes. Always keep your dog under control, especially around wildlife or other hikers.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike this route is typically during spring, early summer, or autumn. These seasons offer more moderate temperatures and generally stable weather. It's advisable to avoid wet conditions, especially for the descent, as the very steep gully can become dangerous. Winter hiking is possible but requires appropriate gear and caution due to potential snow and ice.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Ascent to Monte Pilas — circular from Quecedo de Valdivieso. The trail is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Ascent to Monte Pilas circular route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied and challenging. It begins with relatively simple tracks and paths, but as you gain altitude, it becomes more exposed and inclined, leading to a crest. The descent can involve a very steep gully, which should be avoided in wet conditions, followed by a forested area that can have dense vegetation. The route also traverses the beautiful Desfiladero de las Canalejas.

faqs.question: What notable features or viewpoints can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several notable features. The Ermita de Nuestra Señora de las Pilas is a significant waypoint on a plateau, offering views towards the ridge. The primary objective for many is reaching Monte Pilas (Peña Corva), the highest point of the Sierra de La Tesla, which provides extensive panoramic views. The Desfiladero de las Canalejas is also a beautiful and memorable part of the descent.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. You will find sections that overlap with the MTB Merindad de Valdivielso, Camino de Las Merindades, Sendero de la Tesla, Ruta de los Sentidos, and Sendero de Pilas. It also briefly touches the desfiladero Los Cárcavos and via ferrata Los Cárcavos.

faqs.question: Are there any water sources available on the trail?
faqs.answer: There are no natural water sources mentioned along the route, so it is crucial to carry sufficient water for the entire duration of your hike, especially given its length and difficulty.

faqs.question: What is the recommended strategy for the descent, given its difficulty?
faqs.answer: The descent includes a very steep gully that can be challenging, especially in wet conditions. If you prefer to avoid this more complicated section, you have the option to use the same route for both your ascent and descent. Alternatively, you could drive to the Desfiladero de las Canalejas for a shorter, separate walk through the gorge.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and varied terrain, you should bring appropriate hiking gear, including sturdy hiking boots, layers of clothing, a map or GPS device, sufficient water, snacks, and sun protection. Trekking poles can be very helpful, especially for the steep sections.

faqs.question: How long does the Ascent to Monte Pilas circular hike typically take?
faqs.answer: This challenging hike is estimated to take approximately 6 hours to complete, though this can vary depending on your pace, fitness level, and the time spent enjoying the views.
